strengths: 18+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling
concerns: No evidence of recognized AI expertise or shipped AI/ML products at platform scale — AI involvement is limited to a few months of recent tooling work
Career trajectory is senior/staff individual contributor on product teams; no principal/director-level scope, strategy ownership, or executive/customer advisory experience
No advanced degree in an AI-related field, and cloud experience (AWS CDK, k8s deployments) is applied rather than platform-strategy depth

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI-built products
15+ years engineering experience with entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 18 years of engineering experience spanning freelance (2006) through Staff Engineer at re:cinq, including strong systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec in DLang at Dunnhumby) and founder experience with GISCollective. However, the role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership and recognized AI expertise, and the résumé offers only thin evidence — a few months contributing to MCP code generators and agentic tooling, with no demonstrated AI products, publications, or platform-level AI work. There is also no evidence of director-level influence, advising strategic customers, or setting technical strategy; talks and workshops (FOSS4G, Selenium courses) show knowledge sharing but at a practitioner level. Strong senior/staff engineer, but a poor fit for a principal-level AI strategy role.
